title: pilosella auriculoides subsp. echiogenes
intro: Pilosella auriculoides subsp. echiogenes, commonly known as the False Spleenwort or Yellow Salsify, is a perennial plant species in the Rosaceae family, characterized by its yellow flowers and distinctive leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Pilosella auriculoides subsp. echiogenes include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and act as a natural antiseptic, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
